Job Title: Data Engineer (AWS, Lambda, S3 and Terraform)
Company: Sirius Technology Sydney part of Sirius People Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
We are seeking an ambitious and self-starting Data Engineer to join our client’s team during a high-growth phase. As they kick off Phase 2 delivery, you will play a critical role in maintaining, optimizing, and evolving their AWS platform.
Our client values innovation and efficiency. They are looking for someone who doesn't just "do the job" but actively leverages AI tools to automate workflows and drive results faster.

Please note Melbourne-based candidates with full time works rights only will be considered for this role as it is a requirement for this position and no sponsorship is on offer. 

The Technical Skillset 

To be successful in this role, you will need deep hands-on experience with:

Data Integration: Expert-level development using the Matillion platform to orchestrate complex ETL/ELT workflows.
AWS Data Ecosystem: Proven expertise in S3 (Data Lake architecture), AWS Glue (Cataloging and Jobs), and Lambda (Serverless functions).
Infrastructure as Code (IaC): Robust experience writing and maintaining Terraform and CloudFormation scripts to ensure "Infrastructure as Code" standards.
Scripting & SQL: Advanced SQL for data transformation and Python for Lambda development and automation tasks.
Data Modeling: Solid understanding of Data Warehousing concepts (Star/Snowflake schema) and data partitioning strategies.
Key Responsibilities & Tasks
Pipeline Maintenance: Take ownership of existing Matillion and AWS pipelines, ensuring high availability and resolving bottlenecks.
Phase 2 Delivery: Design and deploy new data features and integrations to support the next stage of the business roadmap.
Automation: Utilize Terraform/CloudFormation to automate the deployment of cloud resources, reducing manual intervention.
Performance Tuning: Optimize SQL queries and AWS Glue jobs to improve processing speed and reduce cloud consumption costs.
Security & Governance: Implement and manage IAM roles, encryption, and data lifecycle policies within the AWS environment.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with the wider team to cross-skill, share knowledge, and ensure architectural alignment.
What You’ll Bring
Innovator’s Mindset: You are enthusiastic about achieving new things and aren't afraid to suggest better ways of working.
AI-Driven Efficiency: You are a modern engineer who leverages AI (LLMs, Copilots) to be as productive and efficient as possible.
Self-Starter Attitude: You can take a high-level brief and run with it, ensuring the platform remains stable and scalable.
Industry Agnostic: Background in any industry is welcome—it's your technical craft and mindset that matter.
The Opportunity

While the initial contract is for 3–6 months, our client has a history of retaining high-performing contractors for up to a year. This is a chance to work in a collaborative, office-based environment where your contributions directly impact the success of a major project phase.

If you are a passionate Data Engineer with experience on AWS S3, Lambda and ideally Matillion, we encourage you to apply today via the link provided! 

Candidates from all backgrounds are welcome to apply too. Please feel free to also send a CV to kgonzalez@siriustechnology.com.au, our Principal IT Consultant for Data, AI and Cyber, thanks.

The Company
Join a small, senior team of software engineers in a well funded company, reporting directly to the engineering co-founders. Own features and help steer the direct of our ML product used by companies in Australia and the UK. And work on a product that users love. 

About You
You're a senior software engineer with extensive experience working on product development and who has built LLM features in production. You're up to date on the latest machine learning techniques (in particular with NLP & LLMs). You enjoy working in small high performing teams and the impact that comes with working in an environment with some ambiguity. Familiarity with AWS Bedrock & Hugging Face ecosystem is a plus. TypeScript and front end experience is also a plus. 

The offer

Remote first company
Flexible work hours (we value output over hours)
Home office budget & professional development program
Five weeks annual leave

About Skillfield

Skillfield is a specialist technology consultancy operating at the intersection of cyber, data and AI. We partner with enterprise clients to protect, transform, and empower their organisations with forward‑thinking solutions. Our reputation is built on outcomes, not effort.

The Opportunity

Contract: 6 months, with potential for extension

Location: Melbourne, Hybrid

We’re seeking a Systems Monitoring Engineer (Zabbix) to operate and enhance our client’s Zabbix‑based monitoring platform. This role focuses on improving visibility, reliability, and alert quality across Linux systems and network infrastructure.

This role is well suited to someone early in their career who has a strong technical foundation, enjoys working with Linux and networks, and is keen to learn how monitoring supports reliable IT systems.

You will work with more experienced engineers to configure monitoring checks, dashboards, and alerts, investigate issues raised by monitoring, and gradually build a strong understanding of our client’s systems and environment.

What You’ll Do

Operate, maintain, and enhance the Zabbix monitoring platform.
Configure and tune monitoring checks, triggers, dashboards, and alerts for Linux servers and network devices.
Investigate alerts, incidents, and performance trends to identify root causes and support resolution.
Work with infrastructure, network, and systems teams to address issues and improve monitoring coverage.
Improve alert quality by reducing noise and ensuring alerts are meaningful and actionable.
Expand monitoring for new and existing systems and services as environments evolve.
Document monitoring standards, procedures, and improvements to support shared understanding.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in system reliability and observability.

What You’ll Bring

Hands‑on experience with IT monitoring tools, ideally Zabbix.
A strong understanding of monitoring metrics for:
Linux/Unix systems (CPU, memory, disk, processes, services)
Network devices (latency, throughput, packet loss, interface health)
Solid Linux administration skills.
Working knowledge of IP networking concepts.
Ability to investigate and troubleshoot technical issues across systems and networks.
An organised and self‑directed approach to learning and working within complex environments.
The ability to collaborate effectively within teams with diverse skill sets and experience levels.

Why Join Skillfield?

Be part of a team serious about doing work that matters.
Join a culture that values clarity, autonomy, and shared success.
Enjoy flexibility, support, and performance‑based recognition.

Ready to make a measurable impact?
